revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chibatake Kofun]][[ja:千畑古墳]]
{{Short description|Burial site in Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Chibatake Kofun | native_name =千畑古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Chibatake Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.140028|N|131.402056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Saito|qid=Q850479|1=Saito, Miyazaki}} | region = [[:en:Kyushu region|Kyushu region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|08|24.1|N|131|24|07.4|E|qq=}}|qq=}} }} {{nihongo|'''Chibatake Kofun'''|千畑古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in Sakurada, Hokita neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|qid=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}} in the [[Kyushu]] region of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|qid=Q34023|lt=Tumulus|1=Tumulus}} was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211610|title= 千畑古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Chibatake Kofun is located on the left bank of the Hitotsuse River, on the southern slope of the Chausubaruhara plateau. On the opposite shore of the river is the Nishitobaru plateau with the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]]. The Chibatake Kofun is part of the Chausuhara Kofun Cluster, which consists of three keyhole-shaped tombs and 52 round Kofuns. There are also more than 50 {{nihongo|corridor-type ''kofun''| 横穴墓|Yokōbo|qq=}} tombs in the area. As the tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], many details about the construction of the mound is uncertain, and dense groves of bamboo forest hamper accurate topographical measurements, but it is thought that the natural topography has been shaped into a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The main [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal stone chamber, which is opened on the south side. The chamber is made of cut stones stacked horizontally and uses huge monolith for the ceiling. This is the largest in the prefecture, with a total length of 9.2 meters. The burial chamber itself has a width of 2.7 meters, a height of 2.8 meters, and length of 1.7 meters. This type of construction is very unusual for the region, and is found elsewhere only at the {{ill|Jōshinzuka Kofun|en|Jōshinzuka Kofun|qid=Q11481383|lt=Jōshinzuka Kofun|1=Jōshinzuka Kofun}} and the Oni no Kutsu Kofun in the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]]. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been recovered, as per an early Meiji period report, local people robbed the tomb during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} to {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|qid=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} era (1804 to 1829) of the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|qid=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.saito.lg.jp/post_274.html Saito City home page] *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1847 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage]
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
